metrics.name: Source Quality
metrics.definition: Primary claims backed by GeneReviews, MedlinePlus, NORD, NCBI StatPearls, OMIM, peer-reviewed reviews

metrics.name: Claim–Citation Density
metrics.definition: Key numerical/clinical claims tagged to named sources; no orphan statistics

metrics.name: Prevalence Accuracy
metrics.definition: OCA3 ~1/8,500 African pops.; overall OCA ~1/17k–20k; ranges and geography noted

metrics.name: Genetics Specificity
metrics.definition: TYRP1, OMIM 203290, autosomal recessive, carrier risks, differential vs syndromic

metrics.name: Phenotype Differentiation
metrics.definition: Rufous/red phenotype vs OCA1/2/4 clearly contrasted with milder ocular phenotype

metrics.name: Age-Staged Watch Signs
metrics.definition: Infancy→toddler→school→adolescence actionable watch items for a 1-year-old

metrics.name: Care Actionability
metrics.definition: Ophthalmology, dermatology, low vision, sun safety, genetics counseling next steps

metrics.name: Data-Backed Visuals
metrics.definition: Charts use literature numbers; captions include source notes and units

metrics.name: Caregiver Usability
metrics.definition: Plain language + precise terms; no scare tactics; hope + realistic expectations

metrics.name: Safety & Scope
metrics.definition: Not medical advice disclaimer; red-flag when-to-call; no cure claims
